annotations.label: Newton's method (root-finding algorithm)

anchor.snippet: is a root-finding algorithm which produces successively better approximations to the roots

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Newton iteration `x ↦ x - P(x)/P'(x)` only for polynomials over a commutative ring, not for arbitrary differentiable real functions.

annotations.label: Geometric interpretation as tangent x-intercept

anchor.snippet: is the x-intercept of the tangent to the graph of f

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration characterizes the Newton iterate as the x-intercept of the tangent line to a real differentiable function.

annotations.label: Tangent-line linear approximation

anchor.snippet: The best linear approximation to an arbitrary differentiable function

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the Fréchet derivative formalism as best linear approximation, but no statement specifically frames it as Newton's tangent-line approximation.

annotations.label: Quadratic convergence at simple root

anchor.snippet: for a root of multiplicity 1, the convergence is at least quadratic

annotations.note: Mathlib has no real-analysis convergence-rate theorem for Newton's method.

annotations.label: Modified algorithm for known multiplicity m

anchor.section: Slow convergence for roots of multiplicity greater than 1
anchor.snippet: if the multiplicity m of the root is known, the following modified algorithm preserves the quadratic convergence rate

annotations.note: No modification of Newton's iteration adjusting for known root multiplicity appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: g(x)=f/f' has simple root at same location

anchor.section: Slow convergence for roots of multiplicity greater than 1
anchor.snippet: will have a root at the same location with multiplicity 1

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state that f/f' converts a multiple root to a simple root.

annotations.label: f(x)=x^2 — linear convergence

anchor.snippet: The function f ( x ) = x 2 has a root at 0

annotations.note: This specific Newton-iteration convergence example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: f(x)=x+x^{4/3} — superlinear subquadratic

anchor.snippet: The function f ( x ) = x + x 4/3 also has a root at 0

annotations.note: This example of Newton iteration is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: f(x)=x^{20}-1 — slow start then quadratic

anchor.snippet: the function f ( x ) = x 20 − 1 has a root at 1

annotations.note: No such Newton-iteration example exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: f(x)=x(1+x^2)^{-1/2} — trichotomy of behavior

anchor.snippet: The function f ( x ) = x (1 + x 2 ) −1/2 has a root at 0

annotations.note: This Newton-method initialization-sensitivity example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subtle initialization dependence with multiple roots

anchor.snippet: has roots at −1, 0, 1, and 3

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: f(x)=x^{1/3} — divergence near root

anchor.section: Divergence even when initialization is close to the root
anchor.snippet: Consider the problem of finding a root of f ( x ) = x 1/3

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: f(x)=x^{1/3}e^{-x^2} — false stopping-criterion failure

anchor.section: Divergence even when initialization is close to the root
anchor.snippet: also has a root at 0

anchor.snippet: the natural logarithm function f ( x ) = ln x has a root at 1

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local convergence with nonzero derivative

anchor.snippet: there exists a neighborhood of α such that for all starting values

annotations.note: Mathlib has no local-convergence theorem for the real Newton iteration.

annotations.label: Quadratic-or-faster convergence under C^2

anchor.snippet: If f is continuously differentiable, its derivative is nonzero at α , and it has a second derivative at α , then the convergence is quadratic or faster

annotations.note: Mathlib has no quadratic convergence theorem for Newton's method on C² functions.

anchor.section: Proof of quadratic convergence for Newton's iterative method
anchor.snippet: the order of convergence is at least quadratic if the following conditions are satisfied

annotations.note: Mathlib has Taylor's theorem but no Newton-method convergence proof using it.

annotations.label: Fourier — Newton iteration converges from left endpoint

anchor.snippet: the Newton iteration x i starting at the left endpoint is monotonically increasing and convergent

annotations.note: Fourier's monotone convergence conditions for Newton iteration are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square root via Newton's method (Babylonian)

anchor.section: Use of Newton's method to compute square roots
anchor.snippet: the problem of finding a number x such that x 2 = a is equivalent to finding a root of the function

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Nat.nthRoot` uses Newton's method internally to compute integer nth roots, but there is no formal proof/statement of the Babylonian sqrt iteration as a real-valued Newton scheme.

annotations.label: Newton's method for k equations via Jacobian inverse

anchor.snippet: left multiply the function F ( x n ) by the inverse of its k × k Jacobian matrix

annotations.note: Mathlib has no multivariate Newton iteration using Jacobian inverse.

annotations.label: Generalized inverse Newton for overdetermined systems

anchor.section: k variables, m equations, with m > k
anchor.snippet: uses the generalized inverse of the non-square Jacobian matrix

annotations.note: Applied numerical example, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basin of attraction for complex zeroes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of basins of attraction for complex Newton iteration.

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Hensel's lemma over ℤ_p, whose proof is built on Newton-iteration construction.
